title: Tunisia at the 2026 World Cup · lineup & formation | MyLineups
original_text:
Drawn into Group F, Tunisia head into the 2026 World Cup in a 3-4-2-1, coached by Hervé Renard.

It is a squad drawn from 25 clubs worldwide, averaging 27.5 years, and worth roughly 44.5 million combined. Plot the XI below, then open it in the free builder to shape your own Tunisia lineup for the tournament.

Tunisia line up for the FIFA World Cup 2026 in Group F in a 3-4-2-1 , with a back 3 and Dahmen in goal.

In goal: Dahmen, Ben Hessen, and Chamakh.

In defence: Abdi, Bronn, Rekik, Talbi, Valery, Arous, Ben Hmida, Chikhaoui, and Neffati.

In midfield: Ben Slimane, Hannibal, Skhiri, Achouri, Ayari, Gharbi, Khedira, and Mahmoud.

In attack: Saad, Tounekti, Ben Ouanes, Chaouat, Elloumi, and Mastouri.

The 26-man squad pulls players from 25 clubs across world football. The starting XI averages 27.5 years and is worth roughly 44.5 million combined.

Hervé Renard (FRA) is the coach guiding this side.

Tunisia stays tied to the World Cup hub and opens directly in the builder if you want to reshape the tournament XI — free on the web or in the mobile app.

Open Tunisia in the free MyLineups builder, reshape the 3-4-2-1, pick your captain and export a clean image.
